title: The Road That Has No End
content:
혻혻혻혻Hast ever tramped along the road
혻혻혻혻That has no end?
혻혻혻혻The far brown winding road, your one
혻혻혻혻Fast friend
혻혻혻혻A tattered weather-beaten swag,
혻혻혻혻A silent mate
혻혻혻혻To send
혻혻혻혻His dumb warm comfort to the heart,
혻혻혻혻A fount where dreams ascend.

혻혻혻혻There占퐏 wondrous freedom on the road
혻혻혻혻That has no end;
혻혻혻혻A man占퐏 heart glows, his spirit leaps
혻혻혻혻To blend
혻혻혻혻Its joy of life with fierce wind占퐏 gust
혻혻혻혻Upon his face:
혻혻혻혻To lend
혻혻혻혻Its cry to Nature占퐏 tumult, full
혻혻혻혻And shrill, as twilight shades descend.

혻혻혻혻The flowers bloom along the road
혻혻혻혻That has no end
혻혻혻혻Cool breezes blow, the gum trees sway
혻혻혻혻And bend;
혻혻혻혻The wild doves woo, and softly coo
혻혻혻혻Their soothing notes,
혻혻혻혻And mend
혻혻혻혻Heart占퐏 throbbing pain to sweet content,
혻혻혻혻And peace lights on the mind占퐏 sad trend

혻혻혻혻There占퐏 pain and toil along the road
혻혻혻혻That has no end;
혻혻혻혻A sinking heart, and weary feet
혻혻혻혻That spend
혻혻혻혻Their strength, and lag and crave respite;
혻혻혻혻And dim tired eyes
혻혻혻혻That tend
혻혻혻혻To close their heavy lids upon
혻혻혻혻The stinging dusts that upward wend.

혻혻혻혻There are sweet still hours along the road
혻혻혻혻That has no end
혻혻혻혻占폧eath twinkling stars when night占퐏 deep shades
혻혻혻혻O占폾rpend;
혻혻혻혻A man占퐏 eyes shine with gathered tears,
혻혻혻혻And memories come
혻혻혻혻To rend
혻혻혻혻His straining heart strings, while above
혻혻혻혻The paling lights his mood commend.

혻혻혻혻I love the road, the swagman占퐏 road
혻혻혻혻That has no end;
혻혻혻혻I love its joys, that pains and toils
혻혻혻혻Transcend;
혻혻혻혻It is my dreams, the life that fills my heart
혻혻혻혻And when death comes and would
혻혻혻혻My peacefulness
혻혻혻혻Amend,
혻혻혻혻I pray that God may let my soul depart
혻혻혻혻With my tattered swag beside me,
혻혻혻혻占폦id my friends that never chide me,
혻혻혻혻And my face towards the distant clouded hill,
혻혻혻혻Where leads the far brown winding road
혻혻혻혻That has no end.
